name: Papua New Guinea

visa_on_arrival.notes: Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023. Easy Visitor Permit (EVP) 60 days. Also MSG member.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023. Also MSG member.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023. Also MSG member.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023.
Pacific Island State; VOA extended December 2023.

French territory; listed on ICA VOA page. MSG associate member (FLNKS). The ICA VOA page note 'Mainland New Zealand, French and United States of America are not eligible for VOA' refers to the sovereign states of New Zealand, France, and USA - not their Pacific territories. New Caledonia appears on the live ICA VOA list.
New Zealand associated territory/Pacific Island jurisdiction; listed on ICA VOA page under 'New Zealand Passport holding Countries and Territories'.
New Zealand associated territory; listed on ICA VOA page under 'New Zealand Passport holding Countries and Territories'.
New Zealand territory; listed on ICA VOA page under 'New Zealand Passport holding Countries and Territories'. Note: Tokelau residents travel on New Zealand passports.
US territory; listed on ICA VOA page under 'United States Territories'. Residents travel on US passports; entry is territory-of-origin based per ICA listing.

US territories; listed on ICA VOA page under 'United States Territories' as a combined entry 'Guam Northern Mariana Islands'. Residents travel on US passports; entry is territory-of-origin based per ICA listing.
French territory; listed on ICA VOA page under 'French Territories of'. Residents travel on French passports; entry is territory-of-origin based per ICA listing.
French territory; listed on ICA VOA page under 'French Territories of'. Residents travel on French passports; entry is territory-of-origin based per ICA listing.

Listed on ICA VOA page as 'Japan (all passport types)'. The September 2023 ICA press release originally specified diplomatic/official/service passport holders only; the live ICA VOA page now shows all passport types. Duration not stated on the ICA VOA page.

Listed on ICA VOA page as 'Indonesia (all Passport types)'. The September 2023 ICA press release originally specified diplomatic/official/service passport holders only; the live ICA VOA page now shows all passport types. Duration not stated on the ICA VOA page.

Listed on ICA VOA page as 'State of Israel (all passport types)'. September 2023 ICA press release PDF confirmed: 'Ordinary passport holders from Israel can get a 60 days single or multiple entry visa on arrival for short stay for business or tourism purposes.' Diplomatic/Official/Service passport holders also eligible.

Listed on live ICA VOA page. Deputy Prime Minister John Rosso announced expansion of VOA to Canada, Singapore, and UK in early March 2025 for up to 60 days, for tourism, leisure, and visiting family purposes. All passport types. Reciprocal basis. ICA VOA page is the authoritative source; the stay duration is confirmed by the March 2025 DPM announcement.

Listed on live ICA VOA page. Deputy Prime Minister John Rosso announced expansion of VOA to Canada, Singapore, and UK in early March 2025 for up to 60 days, for tourism, leisure, and visiting family purposes. All passport types. Reciprocal basis. ICA VOA page is the authoritative source; the stay duration is confirmed by the March 2025 DPM announcement.

Listed on live ICA VOA page. Deputy Prime Minister John Rosso announced expansion of VOA to Canada, Singapore, and UK in early March 2025 for up to 60 days, for tourism, leisure, and visiting family purposes. All passport types. Reciprocal basis. ICA VOA page is the authoritative source; the stay duration is confirmed by the March 2025 DPM announcement.

Listed on ICA VOA page as 'United Arab Emirates (Diplomatic, Special, Mission and Ordinary passports)'. Bilateral MoU on Mutual Exemption of Entry Visa Requirements signed February 4, 2026 at World Governments Summit, Dubai. Reciprocal arrangement: UAE passport holders of all categories may enter PNG for up to 30 days without a visa.

e_visa.notes: eVisa (Easy Visitor Permit and other classes) is available for nationals from eligible VOA countries. Six visa types and 21 visa classes are available online at www.ica.gov.pg. Visa types include: Visitor (Sportsperson, Journalist, EVP 60 days, EVP 30 days, Visiting Relatives, Tourist Own Itinerary, Tourist Tour Package, Yachtperson), Business (Short-term single entry, Short-term multiple entry), Special Exemption (Foreign Official Short Term, Aid Worker, Medical, Designated Events), Entertainer (Comedian, Film maker non-commercial, Film maker commercial, Musician), Restricted Employment Visa (REV), and Working Resident (Consultant Specialist). Processing time: up to 10 working days (can be near-instantaneous if all requirements met). All fees in USD equivalent. Application via www.ica.gov.pg only - no third-party portals.

visa_policy.notes: All foreign visitors to Papua New Guinea require a visa unless eligible for VOA or eVisa. Transit under 7 hours does not require a visa; transit over 7 hours requires a visitor visa. APEC Business Travel Card holders with a 'PNG' code on the reverse side may enter for business trips for up to 59 days (minimum stay per ABTC scheme). Authorized entry ports: Jacksons International Airport (Port Moresby) and Wutung Land Border Post (Vanimo). Australia banned from VOA since March 1, 2014 (NEC decision December 2013). Mainland New Zealand, mainland France, and mainland USA are NOT eligible for VOA per ICA page - however, their respective Pacific territories (Cook Islands/Niue/Tokelau; New Caledonia/French Polynesia/Wallis and Futuna; American Samoa/Guam/Northern Mariana Islands) ARE listed on the ICA VOA page. India and South Korea have signed bilateral visa agreements (May 21 and May 29, 2023 respectively) awaiting parliamentary ratification - current status as of June 2026 unconfirmed. The ICA VOA page references 'PNG-USA Defense Cooperation Agreement' as a note alongside the statement that mainland USA is NOT eligible for standard VOA. PNG Digital Arrival Card (PNGDAC): effective October 1, 2025, all travelers entering Papua New Guinea (including VOA/eVisa holders and PNG citizens) must complete the PNGDAC online at pngdac.ica.gov.pg up to 72 hours before arrival. This is a procedural/border data requirement and does NOT replace or affect VOA/eVisa eligibility. It replaces the former paper arrival card. Source: info.gov.pg official announcements.

conditional_access.conditions: Chinese nationals holding Diplomatic, Official, Service, or Public passports are eligible for VOA. The 2025 ICA handbook states: 'Citizens of China who are Diplomatic, Official, Service and Public passports holders can get Visa on Arrival (VoA) in Papua New Guinea.' The live ICA VOA page specifies 'China (Diplomatic, Official and Service Passport only)' - the 2025 handbook additionally adds 'Public' passport class. Ordinary Chinese passport holders under tour groups are covered separately. Duration not stated on ICA page.

credential.subtype: APEC Business Travel Card (ABTC) with PNG endorsement code on reverse

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must be a passport holder of a participating APEC economy. 19 full participating economies: Australia, Brunei Darussalam, Chile, China, Hong Kong (China), Indonesia, Japan, Republic of Korea, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Papua New Guinea, Peru, Philippines, Russia, Singapore, Chinese Taipei, Thailand, Vietnam. USA and Canada are transitional members. Card must show 'PNG' code on reverse. Valid for business travel only (trade and investment), not tourism. Card valid for 5 years from first issue. Pre-clearance by home APEC economy required. Minimum 59 days stay per visit per ICA APEC page.

credential.subtype: US Defense/Military personnel status under PNG-USA Defense Cooperation Agreement

conditional_access.conditions: Mainland USA passport holders are explicitly NOT eligible for standard VOA per ICA official VOA page. The ICA VOA page lists 'PNG-USA Defense Cooperation Agreement' as a separate note, indicating special entry provisions for US defense/military personnel under the DCA signed at Port Moresby on May 22, 2023, entered into force August 16, 2023. Exact immigration modalities for US defense personnel under the DCA could not be confirmed from official sources - the DCA treaty text on state.gov is image-based. This entry reflects the official ICA page reference only.

credential.subtype: Registered organized tour group with PNG Tourism Promotion Authority

conditional_access.conditions: Ordinary Chinese passport holders travelling in organized tour groups with a registered PNG tour operator are eligible for VOA at no cost. The 2025 ICA handbook states: 'Chinese tourists with ordinary passports travelling in organized tour groups with a registered tour operator in Papua New Guinea are also eligible for VoA.' September 2023 ICA press release PDF confirmed: 'Chinese tourists who holds ordinary passports and travels under registered and organized tour groups are eligible for a 30 days single entry visitor visa at no cost.'

cbi.notes: Papua New Guinea does not have a formal citizenship-by-investment program. Citizenship by naturalization (including for investors and businesspersons) is available after 8+ years of lawful residency. Dual citizenship is permitted with 8 prescribed countries: Australia, New Zealand, Vanuatu, Fiji, Samoa, United Kingdom, Germany, and United States of America. Confirmed in the 2025 ICA Immigration and Entry Permit Handbook.

programs.name: Permanent Residence - Investor/Business Class
programs.notes: Applicant must be the majority owner of a business investment in a registered resident business for at least 5 years with net assets and turnover exceeding K2 million; OR any property investment with a net investment in PNG of at least K10 million. CEO class: Chief Executive Officers of large companies (250+ employees, K10M+ annual turnover, operating 5+ years). Minimum residency of 5 years required before PR application. After 8 years continuous lawful residence, investor may apply for citizenship by naturalization. Confirmed via 2025 ICA handbook general provisions; specific PR fee schedule not re-verified in this session.

programs.name: Restricted Employment Visa (REV)
programs.eligibility: Nationals from VOA-eligible countries; businesses in Oil and Gas, Mining, and major corporate/agricultural companies sponsoring foreign workers with recognised qualifications and skills not available locally. Maximum 4 uses per year.
programs.notes: 30-day single entry per use, maximum 4 times per year. Available online (eVisa) for nationals from eligible countries. Confirmed in 2025 ICA handbook as one of the 21 eVisa classes.

programs.name: APEC Business Travel Card (ABTC) Scheme
programs.eligibility: Passport holders of APEC economies participating in the ABTC scheme who have been pre-approved by their home economy and have PNG code on the reverse of their ABTC.
programs.notes: ABTC holders with 'PNG' code on card may enter PNG without a separate visa for business trips for a minimum of 59 days per visit. Card is valid for 5 years from first issue. For business travel only, not tourism. 19 full participating economies plus USA and Canada as transitional members. Confirmed via live ICA APEC page.

agreements: Melanesian Spearhead Group (MSG) - Fiji, Solomon Islands, Vanuatu (full members); New Caledonia/FLNKS (associate) listed on ICA VOA page individually
Papua New Guinea - United Arab Emirates: MoU on Mutual Exemption of Entry Visa Requirements, signed February 4, 2026 (Dubai, World Governments Summit) - all passport categories, 30 days, reciprocal
Papua New Guinea - Japan: bilateral visa exemption agreement - VOA for all passport types per current ICA VOA page; originally September 2023 for diplomatic/official/service passports only
Papua New Guinea - Indonesia: bilateral visa exemption agreement - VOA for all passport types per current ICA VOA page; originally September 2023 for diplomatic/official/service passports only
Papua New Guinea - Israel: bilateral visa exemption agreement - VOA for all passport types including ordinary passport holders, 60 days, effective September 2023

Papua New Guinea - China (PRC): bilateral visa exemption agreement - VOA for Diplomatic/Official/Service/Public passport holders; ordinary passport holders eligible under registered organized tour groups
Papua New Guinea - India: bilateral visa exemption agreement signed May 21, 2023 - pending parliamentary ratification; status as of June 2026 unconfirmed
Papua New Guinea - South Korea: bilateral visa exemption agreement signed May 29, 2023 - pending parliamentary ratification; status as of June 2026 unconfirmed
Papua New Guinea - USA: Defense Cooperation Agreement signed May 22, 2023, entered into force August 16, 2023 - special entry provisions for US defense/military personnel (separate from standard VOA)
Australia: VOA banned since March 1, 2014 (NEC decision December 2013) - Australian passport holders must obtain visa at PNG overseas mission before travel

validation_notes: CORRECTION (June 2026): Removed New Zealand (NZL) from visa_on_arrival. The live ICA VOA page (scraped June 2026) explicitly states 'Mainland New Zealand, French and United States of America are not eligible for VOA.' The prior record incorrectly listed NZL with a 60-day VOA under the December 2023 Pacific Island States extension. New Zealand passport holders must obtain a visa before travel.

CORRECTION (June 2026): Updated China conditional_access entry to include 'public' passport class. The 2025 ICA handbook states 'Diplomatic, Official, Service and Public passports holders' - prior record only listed diplomatic/official/service.
CORRECTION (June 2026): Updated ABTC max_stay_days from 60 to 59 to match the live ICA APEC page which states 'minimum of 59 days stay each visit'.

CORRECTION (June 2026): Removed the MSG 'bloc' entry from visa_on_arrival. The live ICA VOA page lists individual Pacific states (Fiji, Solomon Islands, Vanuatu, etc.) rather than an 'MSG member countries' umbrella entry. MSG membership is noted in individual country entries and agreements.

CORRECTION (June 2026): Updated Guam and Northern Mariana Islands to a single combined entry 'Guam and Northern Mariana Islands' to match the ICA VOA page rendering ('Guam Northern Mariana Islands') - these were previously two separate entries.
CONFIRMED (June 2026): Australia is correctly absent from VOA list - VOA ban confirmed by official ICA public notice (effective March 1, 2014).
CONFIRMED (June 2026): UAE (ARE) 30-day VOA confirmed via ICA VOA page listing 'United Arab Emirates (Diplomatic, Special, Mission and Ordinary passports)'.
CONFIRMED (June 2026): eVisa updated to reflect 2025 handbook: 6 visa types, 21 visa classes. The /evisa URL on ICA returns 404; the eVisa portal is at evisa.ica.gov.pg.
CONFIRMED (June 2026): Dual citizenship list (Australia, New Zealand, Vanuatu, Fiji, Samoa, United Kingdom, Germany, United States of America) confirmed via 2025 ICA handbook.

NOTE ADDED (June 2026, validation run 2): PNG Digital Arrival Card (PNGDAC) effective October 1, 2025. All travelers must complete PNGDAC online at pngdac.ica.gov.pg up to 72 hours before arrival. Replaces paper arrival card. Does NOT replace or affect VOA/eVisa eligibility. Source: info.gov.pg official announcements (icsa-launches-the-png-digital-arrival-card) and ica.gov.pg official pages. Added to visa_policy.notes.

CONFIRMED (June 2026, validation run 2): India-PNG bilateral visa agreement remains unratified. India MEA bilateral visa exemption page (mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport) does NOT list Papua New Guinea. India-PNG agreement signed May 21, 2023 remains pending parliamentary ratification.
CONFIRMED (June 2026, validation run 2): US citizens must obtain visa before travel (no VOA). Confirmed by US State Department travel.state.gov page for Papua New Guinea.
CONFIRMED (June 2026, validation run 2): UAE 30-day VOA (all passport types) confirmed by pmnec.gov.pg official source - MoU signed February 4, 2026.

NOTE (June 2026, validation run 2): ICA website (ica.gov.pg) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden for all pages during this validation run (including VOA page, press releases, APEC page, eVisa portal). No direct re-confirmation of VOA list possible. Existing VOA entries preserved per preservation-over-guessing rule. No official source indicated any additions or removals since June 2026 validation.

CORRECTION (June 2026, validation run 3): Updated max_stay_days for Canada (CAN), Singapore (SGP), and United Kingdom (GBR) from null to 60. Multiple secondary sources reporting Deputy Prime Minister John Rosso's March 2025 announcement confirm 60-day VOA for these three nationalities. Sources: visaguide.world and travelandtourworld.com both cite 60-day duration for tourism/leisure/family visit purposes. The ICA VOA page (which lists them) is the authoritative official source; the secondary sources provide the duration detail not visible from the ICA page alone due to persistent 403 errors. The ICA website remained HTTP 403 Forbidden during this validation run.

CONFIRMED (June 2026, validation run 3): UAE (ARE) 30-day VOA re-confirmed via info.gov.pg. MoU signed February 4, 2026; covers diplomatic, official, and ordinary passports; up to 30 days; reciprocal. No effective date stated in the announcement article.
CONFIRMED (June 2026, validation run 3): US citizens must obtain visa before travel. Confirmed by US State Department travel.state.gov (no VOA available). No change to existing record.

CONFIRMED (June 2026, validation run 3): India-PNG bilateral visa agreement not yet in force. India MEA (mea.gov.in) does not list PNG in bilateral visa exemption page. No ratification evidence found.

NOTE (June 2026, validation run 3): PNG Embassy in Washington (pngembassy.org) VOA list as of June 2026 only lists the original 2023 set (Pacific Islands + China/Japan/Indonesia/Israel diplomatic/official passport holders). It does not reflect Canada/Singapore/UK or UAE additions, suggesting the Embassy website lags behind the live ICA VOA page. The ICA page (which was previously scraped showing these additions) and official government announcements are the authoritative sources.
gaps: No official PNG immigration source confirms any conditional entry rule granting VOA/visa-free access to holders of US, UK, Australian, Schengen, or other third-country visas or residence permits. All VOA/bilateral access rules are nationality-based (passport), not credential-based.

PNG-USA Defense Cooperation Agreement (signed May 22, 2023; in force August 16, 2023) is referenced on the ICA VOA page as a note alongside the explicit statement that mainland USA is NOT eligible for standard VOA. The DCA treaty text (state.gov PDF) is image-based and unreadable; exact immigration modalities for US defense/military personnel under the DCA are unconfirmed from official sources.
Max stay days for Japan and Indonesia bilateral VOA not explicitly stated on the current ICA VOA page (UK, Canada, Singapore confirmed at 60 days from March 2025 DPM announcement).

No official ICA press release was found specifically announcing Canada, Singapore, and United Kingdom additions to the VOA list. The 60-day duration was confirmed via secondary media reports of Deputy PM John Rosso's announcement (~March 5, 2025). A direct official ICA press release URL could not be found due to ICA website returning HTTP 403.

India and South Korea bilateral visa agreements confirmed signed (May 2023) but pending parliamentary ratification - current ratification status as of June 2026 unconfirmed from official sources. India MEA bilateral visa exemption page (mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport, fetched June 2026) does NOT list Papua New Guinea, confirming the agreement is not yet in force.

The live ICA VOA page lists Indonesia and Japan as 'all passport types' (confirmed from prior June 2026 scrape) - the exact date ordinary passport holders of Japan and Indonesia became eligible (vs only diplomatic/official/service as per the original September 2023 press release) is not documented in any official press release found. Secondary media (PNG Business News LinkedIn) confirms Japan expanded to all passport types.
No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official source.
RBI minimum investment amounts are in PGK (Papua New Guinea Kina); specific fee schedule not re-verified in this session.
Tokelau residents travel on New Zealand passports; the ICA listing of 'Tokelau' as a separate VOA-eligible entry does not specify what travel document holders would present at the border.

The ICA VOA page does not state whether Cook Islands, Niue, and Tokelau nationals travelling on New Zealand passports are eligible (since NZL is explicitly not eligible) or if only Cook Islands/Niue passports (issued by NZ) qualify. This distinction is unresolved from official sources.

ICA website (ica.gov.pg) returned HTTP 403 for all pages during June 2026 validation runs (runs 2 and 3). The live VOA list could not be directly re-confirmed. Existing VOA entries preserved from prior validated scrape (June 2026 session run 1 via Firecrawl stealth proxy). Any additions or removals to the VOA list since that scrape cannot be confirmed without direct site access.

PNG Digital Arrival Card (PNGDAC): now mandatory for all arrivals from October 1, 2025. The PNGDAC requirement is a procedural/border entry requirement separate from visa eligibility. The pngdac.ica.gov.pg portal also returned 403 during this session; information sourced from info.gov.pg official announcements.

UAE VOA effective date: the MoU was signed February 4, 2026, but the info.gov.pg announcement does not specify an implementation/effective date. The UAE entry is listed on the ICA VOA page (per prior scrape) confirming it is in force, but the exact date of entry into force is unconfirmed from official sources.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa – Own Itinerary
visa_types.purpose: For individuals who wish to sightsee independently, travel, or visit friends in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Visitor Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50. Initial application fee is Nil; extension fee USD 150; late extension USD 700. Apply at www.ica.gov.pg. Requires request letter, passport bio page copy, and travel itinerary with onward/return ticket.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa – Tour Package
visa_types.purpose: For group tours, student excursion groups, and other organised tour parties visiting Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Visitor Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50. Sponsor/tour agent must be registered with PNG Tourism Promotion Authority (TPA) and Investment Promotion Authority (IPA). Requires letter of request from registered tour agent, passport bio page, and travel itinerary.

visa_types.name: Visiting Relative Visa
visa_types.purpose: For individuals visiting husbands, wives, or other family members residing in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Visitor Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50. Extension fee USD 150; late extension USD 700. Requires request letter, passport bio page, onward/return ticket, and financial guarantee.

visa_types.name: Easy Visitor Permit (EVP) – 30 Days
visa_types.purpose: For eligible nationality holders wishing to visit PNG for holidays, sightseeing, social or recreational activities, or to visit relatives and friends, for up to 30 days.
visa_types.notes: Visitor Visa class. Available to passport holders from VOA-eligible countries. Single entry, valid 30 days from date of arrival. Not extendable. eVisa fee USD 50. Also obtainable on arrival at Jacksons International Airport. Check eligibility at www.ica.gov.pg.

visa_types.name: Easy Visitor Permit (EVP) – 60 Days
visa_types.purpose: For eligible nationality holders wishing to visit PNG for holidays, sightseeing, social or recreational activities, or to visit relatives and friends, for up to 60 days.
visa_types.notes: Visitor Visa class. Available to passport holders from VOA-eligible countries. Single entry, valid 60 days from date of arrival. Extension fee USD 150; late extension USD 700. eVisa fee USD 50. Also obtainable on arrival at Jacksons International Airport. Check eligibility at www.ica.gov.pg.

visa_types.name: Yachtperson Visa (Owner/Captain/Crew)
visa_types.purpose: For the owner and/or captain of a yacht, as well as any accompanying spouse and/or children, arriving by sea.
visa_types.notes: Visitor Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50. Extension fee USD 150 (owner/captain), USD 200 (crew); late extension USD 700. Requires request letter from owner/captain, passport bio page, and yacht particulars (optional).

visa_types.name: Sportsperson Visa
visa_types.purpose: For individuals taking part in representative sporting events or codes in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires letter from PNG-based sponsor, passport bio page, onward/return ticket, and prescribed migration fee.

visa_types.name: Journalist/Reporter Visa
visa_types.purpose: For journalists, reporters, TV presenters, announcers, camerapersons, photographers, and media correspondents visiting Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Visitor Visa class. Extension fee USD 500; late extension USD 1,050. eVisa fee USD 50. Requires prior clearance letter from the Prime Minister's Media Unit before applying. Not available online without pre-clearance. Passport bio page, travel itinerary with onward/return ticket required.

visa_types.name: Business Visa – Short-Term Multiple Entry
visa_types.purpose: For persons making multiple business visits during a single 12-month period, including for meetings, conferences, exploratory visits, or business negotiations.
visa_types.notes: Business Visa class. Open to all nationalities. eVisa fee USD 50. Extension fee USD 500; late extension USD 1,000. Requires letter from PNG-based sponsor, passport bio page, prescribed migration fee, and return/ongoing ticket.

visa_types.name: Business Visa – Short-Term Single Entry
visa_types.purpose: For persons entering PNG for a single business visit of up to 30 days for meetings, conferences, exploratory visits, or business negotiations.
visa_types.notes: Business Visa class. Not extendable. eVisa (eVisa) available for nationals from eligible countries. eVisa fee USD 50. Requires letter from PNG-based sponsor, passport bio page, prescribed migration fee, and return/ongoing ticket. Check eligibility at www.ica.gov.pg.

visa_types.name: Restricted Employment Visa (REV)
visa_types.purpose: Allows businesses to sponsor foreign workers with recognised qualifications and experience in fields not available locally, primarily in Oil & Gas, Mining, and major corporate or agricultural companies.
visa_types.notes: Business Visa class. 30-day single entry per use; not extendable; maximum 4 uses per year. Available online (eVisa) for nationals from eligible countries. eVisa fee USD 50. Requires cover letter from PNG-based sponsor, passport bio page, prescribed migration fee, and proof of return ticket.

visa_types.name: Work Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals employed in the private sector in Papua New Guinea requiring formal work authorisation.
visa_types.notes: Working Resident Employment Visa. Application must be submitted by the employer through the Sponsor Portal at www.ica.gov.pg. Extension fee USD 700; late extension USD 1,350. Requires: employee passport bio page, CV, job description, certified qualifications, signed employment contract, two colour passport photos, right thumb print, professional registration evidence, IPA Certificate of Incorporation, police clearance, medical report including X-ray, and Maintenance Guarantee Bond signed by employer. HIV test required for applicants over 16 years of age for long-term stays.

visa_types.name: Working Resident – Consultant/Specialist
visa_types.purpose: For consultants and specialists providing services in large corporate, Oil & Gas, and Mining companies for short stays of up to 90 days.
visa_types.notes: Working Resident Employment Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50 (eVisa available). Extension fee USD 700; late extension USD 1,350. Requires letter from PNG-based company/sponsor, passport bio page, prescribed fee, proof of onward/return ticket, Maintenance Guarantee Bond completed by sponsor, and copy of Contract/Service Agreement.

visa_types.name: Working Resident – Short-Term Employment
visa_types.purpose: For organisations wishing to employ a foreign national for a term of 6 months or less.
visa_types.notes: Working Resident Employment Visa class. Extension fee USD 700; late extension USD 1,350. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter signed at managerial level, IPA Company Form 4 and 5, completed and signed application form, Maintenance Guarantee Bond, and passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Business Person/Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For business persons or investors who want to do business and invest in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Working Resident Employment Visa class. Extension fee USD 700; late extension USD 1,350. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter, completed application form, Maintenance Guarantee Bond, and passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Working Dependent Visa
visa_types.purpose: Extension visa for dependents previously under Working Resident Dependent status who wish to seek employment in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Working Resident Employment Visa class (Change of Status). Extension fee USD 700; late extension USD 1,350. Applicable when: legally dependent spouse with teaching/medical/other essential qualification decides to obtain employment; person has changed marital status while in PNG; person completed employment obligations and seeking new employer; or company name change. Requirements differ by COS category - see www.ica.gov.pg.

visa_types.name: Dependent Visa (Working Resident)
visa_types.purpose: For non-citizens who are biological family members (including spouses, children, and aged/de facto partners) of a principal Working Resident visa holder.
visa_types.notes: Working Resident Employment Visa class. Extension fee USD 700; late extension USD 1,350. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter, completed application form, passport bio page, certified principal visa page, birth certificate, and marriage certificate (for spouse). De facto relationships require statutory declarations from both parties.

visa_types.name: Entertainer Visa – Film Maker (Commercial)
visa_types.purpose: For non-citizens intending to make a commercial film or documentary in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Entertainer Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50 (eVisa available). Extension fee USD 700; late extension USD 1,350. Requires approval from PNG National Film Institute (NFI), clearance letters from PNG Department of Foreign Affairs and the relevant Provincial Administration, and clearance from PNG Prime Minister's Office Media Unit.

visa_types.name: Entertainer Visa – Film Maker (Non-Commercial)
visa_types.purpose: For individuals who wish to make not-for-profit or public release films in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Entertainer Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50 (eVisa available). Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires clearance from PNG National Film Institute and PNG Prime Minister's Office Media Unit.

visa_types.name: Entertainer Visa – Comedian (Commercial)
visa_types.purpose: For persons providing entertainment or advertisements as comedians for commercial purposes in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Entertainer Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50 (eVisa available). Extension fee USD 500; late extension USD 1,050. Requires sponsor letter from event organiser, passport bio page, prescribed fee, and return/ongoing ticket.

visa_types.name: Entertainer Visa – Musician (Commercial)
visa_types.purpose: For individuals who wish to tour Papua New Guinea to perform live music or for recording purposes, commercially.
visa_types.notes: Entertainer Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50 (eVisa available). Extension fee USD 500; late extension USD 1,050. Requires sponsor letter from event organiser, passport bio page, prescribed fee, and return/ongoing ticket.

visa_types.name: Entertainer Visa – Gospel Group (Charity)
visa_types.purpose: For gospel groups doing performances and shows for charity purposes in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Entertainer Visa class. Not available online - manual application required. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires sponsor letter from event organiser, duly completed application form, passport bio page, prescribed fee, and return/ongoing ticket.

visa_types.name: Entertainer Visa – Cultural Group (Charity)
visa_types.purpose: For cultural groups wishing to travel to Papua New Guinea to do charity work or showcase their country of origin.
visa_types.notes: Entertainer Visa class. Not available online - manual application required. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires sponsor letter from sponsoring organisation, duly completed and signed application form, passport bio page, prescribed fee, and return/ongoing ticket.

visa_types.name: Student Visa – Formal Education
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who wish to study in Papua New Guinea on exchange programmes or by self-invitation at registered educational institutions.
visa_types.notes: Student Visa class. Applications are submitted manually (not online). Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter, acceptance letter from school Registrar, duly completed and signed application form, passport bio page, and prescribed fee.

visa_types.name: Student Visa – Occupational Trainee
visa_types.purpose: For students sponsored by institutions to undertake training with government organisations, companies, or under individual training/programme circumstances in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Student Visa class. Applications are submitted manually (not online). Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Visa consideration: non-extendable, valid 6 months to 1 year. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter, completed and signed application form, passport bio page, and prescribed fee.

visa_types.name: Student Dependent Visa
visa_types.purpose: For family members (children, spouse, de facto partner) who wish to accompany the principal student visa applicant during their stay in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Student Visa class. Applications are submitted manually (not online). Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires sponsor letter, completed and signed application form, passport bio page, prescribed fee. If lodged separately from principal applicant, must provide certified copies of principal's passport bio page and valid visa.

visa_types.name: Special Exemption Visa – Foreign Officials (Long Term)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign government and international organisation employees without diplomatic or official status who are conducting business on behalf of their government or international organisations, on a long-term basis.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Fee: Nil. Extension fee: Nil. Requires sponsor letter from foreign government or international organisation (Diplomatic Note), clearance letter from PNG Department of Foreign Affairs (Diplomatic Note), completed and signed application form, and copy of valid Official Passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Special Exemption Visa – Foreign Officials (Short Term)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign national employees without diplomatic or official status conducting short-term business on behalf of their government.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Fee: Nil. eVisa fee USD 50. eVisa application available online. Requires sponsoring letter from foreign government/international organisation (Diplomatic Note), clearance letter from PNG Department of Foreign Affairs (Diplomatic Note), and copy of valid Official Passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Diplomat Visa
visa_types.purpose: For Diplomatic Passport holders of foreign governments who conduct official negotiations and maintain political, economic, and social relations with Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Fee: Nil. Extension: Nil. Requires sponsor letter from Foreign Mission (Diplomatic Note), clearance letter from PNG Department of Foreign Affairs, completed and signed application form, and copy of Diplomatic Passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Aid Worker/Volunteer Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who work for an international organisation providing humanitarian or development assistance to the citizens of Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. eVisa fee USD 50 (YWAM Aid Worker variant also available online). Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter signed at managerial level, work permit (if applicable), completed application form, and passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Research Academic Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals undertaking educational, medical, or scientific research in Papua New Guinea, or teaching in a PNG institution.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter, approval from PNG National Research Institute, Institute of Medical Research, or educational institution stating purpose and duration, completed application form, and passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Religious Worker Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign religious workers who are members of a national religious denomination in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter, completed application form, and passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Emergency Relief Worker Visa
visa_types.purpose: For humanitarian aid workers intending to assist in humanitarian crises caused by natural or man-made disasters in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires clearance letter from PNG National Disaster Office, completed application form, and passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Medical Workers Visa
visa_types.purpose: For doctors or trainees entering Papua New Guinea for short-term placements in hospitals, valid for periods up to six months.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires clearance letter from the PNG Medical Board, letter from sponsor, completed application form, and passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Melanesian Spearhead Group (MSG) Visa
visa_types.purpose: For passport holders of Melanesian Spearhead Group member countries visiting Papua New Guinea for up to 30 days.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Fee: Nil. Extension: Nil. Only applicable to passport holders of MSG member countries (Fiji, Solomon Islands, Vanuatu - PNG is a founding member). Valid for stays of 30 days or less. Obtained on arrival. Requires incoming Passenger Arrival Card, valid passport bio page, and travel itinerary with onward/return ticket.

visa_types.name: Domestic Worker Visa (Accredited Diplomats)
visa_types.purpose: For domestic workers of foreign nationals approved by their government to conduct official negotiations and maintain relations with Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires sponsor letter from Foreign Mission, clearance letter from PNG Department of Foreign Affairs, completed application form, and passport bio page.

visa_types.name: Dependent Visa (Diplomat/International Organisation)
visa_types.purpose: For spouses and aged parents (non-working) of a Diplomat or non-citizen employee of an international organisation posted in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Fee: Nil. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter, completed application form, and passport bio page. If lodged without principal applicant, must provide certified copies of principal's valid passport page and valid visa.

visa_types.name: Dependent of Citizen (Non-Working)
visa_types.purpose: For children of Papua New Guinea citizens who are foreign nationals and wish to reside non-working in Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Extension fee USD 75; late extension USD 380. Requires undertaking/sponsor letter signed at managerial level, completed application form, passport bio pages for applicant and both parents, prescribed fee, birth certificate of child, marriage certificate for spouse, and statutory declarations for de facto relationships.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence – Long-Term Resident
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have lawfully resided in Papua New Guinea for a minimum of 5 years and meet eligibility criteria including CEOs, investors, skilled professionals, missionaries, or retirees.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence class. Eligibility: Minimum 5 years lawful residency in PNG; must hold a current PNG entry permit; working knowledge of English or a PNG language. Eligible categories: (1) CEOs of companies with 250+ employees and K10M+ annual turnover operating 5+ years; (2) Majority owners of a registered business with net assets/turnover >K2M or property investment >K10M in PNG for 5+ years; (3) Skilled professionals (medical doctors, specialists, educationists) with tertiary qualifications and 5+ years in PNG; (4) Missionaries/religious workers with 5+ years in PNG and K25,000+/year income or written guarantee; (5) Retirees over age 55 with K50,000+/year income who worked in PNG 5+ years; (6) Legally married spouse of PNG citizen for 5+ years. After 8+ years, can apply for citizenship by naturalization. Dependent PR fee covered by principal.

visa_types.name: Transit (No Visa Required – Under 7 Hours)
visa_types.purpose: Airside transit through Papua New Guinea for passengers with a total waiting time of less than 7 hours.
visa_types.notes: No visa required for transit passengers with less than 7 hours waiting time. Transit for over 7 hours requires a visitor visa. Confirmed in 2025 ICA handbook general information section.

visa_types.name: Transit Visitor Visa (Over 7 Hours)
visa_types.purpose: For passengers transiting through Papua New Guinea with a waiting time exceeding 7 hours who must leave the international zone.
visa_types.notes: ICA handbook recommends applying for a visitor visa online if transit time exceeds 7 hours. Standard Visitor Visa application fees apply (Nil base fee; eVisa USD 50). Apply at www.ica.gov.pg in advance.

visa_types.name: Designated Events Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals attending designated events approved by the Government of Papua New Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Special Exemption Visa class. Listed as one of the 21 eVisa classes available online. Specific fee not separately itemised in the 2025 handbook fee schedule under Special Exemption (likely Nil or standard Special Exemption fee applies). For government-approved events only.

outbound_official.note: no official Papua New Guinea government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

sources_checked.description: ICA Visa on Arrival page - live official list of VOA eligible countries
sources_checked.note: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl stealth proxy June 2026. Current list confirmed: Canada, China (Diplomatic/Official/Service only), UAE (all categories), FSM, Fiji, Indonesia (all types), Japan (all types), Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Nauru, New Caledonia, Palau, Samoa, Singapore, Solomon Islands, State of Israel (all types), Tonga, Tuvalu, UK, Vanuatu; French Territories (French Polynesia, Wallis and Futuna); New Zealand Passport holding territories (Cook Islands, Niue, Tokelau); US Territories (American Samoa, Guam Northern Mariana Islands). Note: 'Mainland New Zealand, French and United States of America are not eligible for VOA' and 'PNG-USA Defense Cooperation Agreement' noted separately.

sources_checked.description: ICA Immigration and Entry Permit Handbook Papua New Guinea 2025 - official PDF
sources_checked.note: Full first 10 pages extracted via Firecrawl PDF parser June 2026. Confirms 6 visa types and 21 classes for eVisa; confirms China Public passport eligible for VOA; confirms dual citizenship with 8 prescribed countries; confirms transit rules (under 7 hours no visa needed).

sources_checked.description: ICA Press Release PDF: VOA announcement September 25, 2023
sources_checked.note: Previously scraped. Key verbatim text: diplomatic/official/service passport holders from Japan, Israel, Indonesia, and China eligible; ordinary Israeli passport holders get 60-day VOA; ordinary Chinese passport holders in organized tour groups get 30-day free VOA.

sources_checked.description: ICA APEC Business Travel Card Scheme page
sources_checked.note: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl June 2026. Confirms 19 participating economies; minimum 59 days stay per visit; pre-clearance required; USA and Canada are transitional members.

sources_checked.description: National Information Center (info.gov.pg): PNG-UAE Visa Waiver Agreement announcement
sources_checked.note: Official PNG government domain. Confirms MoU signed February 4, 2026 at World Governments Summit, Dubai. Covers diplomatic, official, and ordinary passports. Up to 30 days, reciprocal.

sources_checked.description: PNG Department of Prime Minister and NEC: PNG-UAE bilateral visa waiver agreement
sources_checked.note: Official PNG government domain. Confirms February 4, 2026 signing, all passport categories, 30 days, reciprocal arrangement.

sources_checked.description: ICA Public Notice: Ban of VOA Facility for Australian Passport Holders
sources_checked.note: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl June 2026. NEC decision December 2013; ban effective March 1, 2014. Covers all Australian passport categories (Ordinary, Official, Diplomatic). Australian cruise ship passengers are exempt from the ban.

sources_checked.description: ICA Entry Permit Booklet (Dubai/Online version) - older COVID-era document
sources_checked.note: Successfully scraped via Firecrawl June 2026. This is an older COVID-era booklet (VOA suspended during COVID). Content superseded by 2025 handbook for current policy.

sources_checked.description: PNG Department of Prime Minister and NEC: PNG-UAE bilateral visa waiver agreement
sources_checked.note: Successfully fetched June 2026. Confirms MoU signed February 4, 2026; covers diplomatic, official, and ordinary passports for both countries; up to 30 days stay; reciprocal arrangement. No implementation effective date stated in the article.

sources_checked.description: National Information Center (info.gov.pg): ICSA launches PNG Digital Arrival Card
sources_checked.note: Successfully fetched June 2026. Confirms PNGDAC launched October 2, 2025. Replaces paper arrival card. All travelers must complete at pngdac.ica.gov.pg up to 72 hours before arrival. No visa policy changes announced alongside PNGDAC launch.

sources_checked.description: US State Department: Papua New Guinea International Travel Information
sources_checked.note: Successfully fetched June 2026. Confirms US citizens must obtain visa (physical or eVisa) in advance of arrival - no visa on arrival mentioned for US citizens. Consistent with existing record.

sources_checked.description: India Ministry of External Affairs: Bilateral Visa Exemption Agreements (all passport holders)
sources_checked.note: Successfully fetched June 2026. Papua New Guinea is NOT listed among countries with operational visa exemption agreements with India. Confirms the India-PNG bilateral visa agreement signed May 21, 2023 is still NOT in force as of June 2026.

sources_checked.description: ICA Visa on Arrival page - re-validation attempt June 2026
sources_checked.note: Returned HTTP 403 Forbidden during June 2026 re-validation run. No direct re-confirmation of VOA list possible. Existing entries preserved from prior validated scrape.
